# Dummy Human Genome

The dummy human genome reference was announced in 2003. Base-calling accuracy
reached 99.9% across the finished sequence. The haploid genome spans 3.2 Gb.

The program cost about $2.7 billion in total. The assembly contains
approximately 20,500 protein-coding genes.

An unrelated dummy figure of 42% is deliberately not supported by the source,
used to exercise the unmatched-claim path.
